assessment_items.question_content:
**Simone Martini was a student of [[☃ dropdown 1]] and was well-versed in the Sienese styles, with particular attention to detail, patterns, and decorative elements.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/255442f72a5c0d8a4346b044f12b3b6c.jpg)
hints: **Simone Martini was also Duccio's student and was well-versed in the Sienese styles, with particular attention to detail, patterns, and decorative elements.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**The lilies in the vase were symbolic of Mary's [[☃ dropdown 1]]. Gabriel holds [[☃ dropdown 2]], a symbol of Christ and of peace. The Holy Spirit appears in the form of a [[☃ dropdown 3]].**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/255442f72a5c0d8a4346b044f12b3b6c.jpg)
hints: **The lilies in the vase were symbolic of Mary's virginity. Gabriel holds an olive branch, a symbol of Christ and of peace. The Holy Spirit appears in the form of a dove.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Simone Martini is trying to represent both the divinity and humanity of the event, though previous artists focused on [[☃ dropdown 1]].**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/255442f72a5c0d8a4346b044f12b3b6c.jpg)
hints: **Simone Martini is trying to represent both the divinity and humanity of the event, though previous artists focused on divinity.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**In this altarpiece, the archangel Michael announces to St. John the Baptist that he will baptize Jesus.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/255442f72a5c0d8a4346b044f12b3b6c.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: **This is false. The Angel Gabriel announces to Mary that she's going to have a baby.**
